pi_items.source: Web search for 'APC Smart-UPS SRT 3000VA specifications': Official Schneider Electric USA documentation and multiple retailers confirm this is a real product, model SRT3000RMXLA

privileged_info_summary: The task uses a synthetic but realistic data manifest containing 14 rack-mounted equipment items across 10 target hostnames. The manifest correctly references real products: Cisco Catalyst 9300-48P switches (2 units), Dell PowerEdge R660 1U servers (2 units), Dell PowerEdge R760 2U servers (4 units), and APC Smart-UPS SRT 3000VA units (2 units). However, the manifest contains a factual error: it specifies the APC UPS units as 3U when the actual product is 2U. The metadata includes 'schneider' as an APC keyword, but this word does not appear in the manifest data itself (though APC is owned by Schneider Electric). All file paths (manifest, drawio, PNG) are correctly specified. The equipment supports 5 color categories (Network, Compute, Database, Power, Passive) which exceeds the minimum of 3. The manifest contains 14 distinct items, exceeding the minimum shape requirement of 12.
